sentences.en: Somehow, our recess turned into an actual baseball league this week, complete with two teams and a very serious point system.
sentences.zh: 不知怎麼地，我們這星期的下課時間變成了一個真正的棒球聯盟，有兩支隊伍，還有一套非常認真的計分制度。

sentences.en: Bernice brought an old ball and bat from home, and within two days, half the class had joined one team or the other.
sentences.zh: Bernice 從家裡帶來一顆舊球跟一支球棒，兩天內全班一半的人都加入了某一隊。

sentences.en: I am not naturally strong or fast, so my team put me in a position where I mostly just stand and watch, which I honestly do not mind.
sentences.zh: 我天生不強壯也不快，所以我的隊伍把我放在一個大部分時間只要站著看的位置，老實說我不介意。

sentences.en: William heard about our league and demanded his own school start one too, insisting that seven-year-olds can definitely handle a real bat.
sentences.zh: William 聽說了我們的聯盟，堅持他自己的學校也要辦一個，還堅稱七歲小孩絕對能拿真正的球棒。

sentences.en: The tallest boy in our class turned out to be surprisingly bad at baseball, while one of the shortest girls could hit the ball the farthest every single time.
sentences.zh: 我們班最高的男生打棒球意外地很爛，而其中一個最矮的女生每次都能把球打得最遠。

sentences.en: Our first real game ended in a tie, which somehow made both teams argue even more than if one side had actually lost.
sentences.zh: 我們第一場正式比賽以平手收場，這反而讓兩隊吵得比真的輸掉還更兇。

sentences.en: Bernice challenged the other team to a rematch, announcing loudly that our team would attack from the very first turn this time.
sentences.zh: Bernice 向對方隊伍下戰帖，大聲宣布這次我們隊從第一輪就要展開猛攻。

sentences.en: During the rematch, our team actually won, mostly thanks to the short girl hitting the ball clean over the fence.
sentences.zh: 重賽的時候我們隊真的贏了，主要都要感謝那個矮個子女生把球乾淨俐落地打過圍牆。

sentences.en: The whole thing became so popular that even a few teachers stopped by during recess just to watch our very unofficial league.
sentences.zh: 整件事變得很受歡迎，連幾位老師都在下課時間過來看我們這個超級非正式的聯盟。

sentences.en: One teacher joked that our league was more organized than some actual school sports events he had seen before.
sentences.zh: 有位老師開玩笑說我們的聯盟比他以前看過的一些正式校內運動賽事還要有組織。

sentences.en: William, still jealous he could not officially join, practiced hitting a ball with a bat in our backyard every single evening this week.
sentences.zh: William 還是很嫉妒自己不能正式加入，這星期每天傍晚都在後院拿球棒練習打球。

sentences.en: By Friday, he could actually hit the ball a decent distance, and he immediately declared himself ready for any team, anywhere, anytime.
sentences.zh: 到了星期五，他真的能把球打到還算不錯的距離，他馬上宣布自己隨時隨地都能加入任何一隊。

sentences.en: Our whole recess league plans to keep going until the end of the semester, unless a teacher finally decides it is too loud and too competitive.
sentences.zh: 我們整個下課聯盟打算一直辦到學期末，除非有老師終於受不了覺得太吵太競爭。

sentences.en: Honestly, I think I will be a little sad when it ends, even though my main job on the team is basically just standing there looking successful.
sentences.zh: 老實說，等它結束的時候我大概會有點難過，就算我在隊上的主要工作基本上就是站在那邊看起來很成功的樣子。

quiz.question: What is the surprising result about the tallest boy in class?
options: He is surprisingly bad at baseball.
He hits the ball the farthest.

quiz.question: How does the first game between the two teams end?
What does William do every evening this week to prepare?
options: Practices hitting a ball with a bat in the backyard.
Asks Coco to teach him at school.
